reasoning: The empirical base rate for 'any-increase' ACLED questions is 0.225, which reflects the highly right-skewed nature of conflict datasets (where a few high-activity months inflate the annual mean, meaning that a random future month is mathematically likely to fall below the annual average). In Ivory Coast, the presidential elections scheduled for October 2025 likely created a high pre-election baseline of 'Strategic developments' (averaging 4.75 per month), driven by troop movements, arrests, and political maneuvering. The target 30-day window (late December 2025 to late January 2026) falls during the post-election stabilization phase. Unless there is significant, protracted post-election instability or a major jihadist spillover from Burkina Faso, it is highly likely that event counts will regress to a lower, quieter median level by January 2026. Thus, I expect a probability slightly below the base rate, as the comparative baseline relies on an inflated election-year average.
